verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the beta subunit of mitochondrial ATP synthase (F1 complex), localized to the inner mitochondrial membrane facing the matrix. While ectopic surface ATP synthase has been reported on some cancer/endothelial cells as a contextual phenomenon, this is debated and not a robust gene-specific surface display mechanism. Checking contextual buckets: cell_state_induced — no robust validated surface induction; tissue_restricted_surface — not lineage-restricted to surface; lysosomal_exocytosis — not lysosomal; dual_localization — ectopic surface ATP synthase reports exist but mechanism unclear and contested; stable_surface_attachment — not secreted. Dominant biology is mitochondrial internal.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Ectopic cell-surface ATP synthase (including beta subunit) has been reported on tumor/endothelial cells as angiostatin/apoA-I receptor — could justify contextual/dual_localization.
